name: Chi-square Test
applicable_scenarios: test independence of two categorical variables from a contingency table
goodness-of-fit between observed counts and a theoretical distribution
categorical A/B testing with counts rather than means
typical_cases: association between gender and voting preference
check whether dice rolls deviate from uniform
click-through rates across several ad variants
common_pitfalls: requires expected cell counts >= 5 in most cells; otherwise use Fisher's exact
categorical variables must be mutually exclusive and exhaustive
large samples make even trivial differences significant — report effect size (Cramer's V)
not appropriate for ordinal / paired categorical data
